content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Fix Deployment Issues (Student Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment failures. Learn how to use AI-driven debugging with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first student project successfully.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures usually stem from environment mismatches or dependency conflicts. By leveraging AI-driven debugging with tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate log analysis, identify root causes, and generate fix scripts instantly. This systematic approach transforms confusing error logs into actionable code changes, ensuring your application goes live.
content.intro:
The terminal screen glows with a cascade of red text—a 'Deployment Failed' status that feels like a wall standing between you and your first real-world project. You've written the logic, polished the UI, and felt the thrill of a working build, only to be stopped cold by a cryptic configuration error. It is a rite of passage for every developer, but it doesn't have to be the end of your journey.

Today, we stop guessing. By integrating AI into your deployment pipeline, you transition from 'trial and error' to 'precision engineering.' Using Antigravity to map your infrastructure and Claude Code to surgically resolve dependency issues, you will turn those intimidating error logs into a roadmap for success. Let’s get your code out of your local machine and into the world.

steps.title: Automated Log Analysis
steps.description: Feed your raw deployment logs into the AI to identify specific failure points rather than manually scanning thousands of lines of code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the following deployment log output. Identify the primary cause of failure, distinguish between environment-specific errors and code-logic errors, and suggest a prioritized list of fixes.

steps.title: Dependency Audit
steps.description: Ensure your package versions match the target production environment to prevent 'it works on my machine' syndrome.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare my local package.json dependencies against the production environment requirements. Generate a list of conflicting versions that could cause deployment failure.

steps.title: Environment Variable Validation
steps.description: Deployment often fails due to missing secrets. Use AI to check if your .env setup matches your deployment platform expectations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my current .env.example and deployment config. Identify which environment variables are likely missing or incorrectly formatted for a standard cloud provider setup.

steps.title: Build Script Refinement
steps.description: Optimize your build commands to ensure they are deterministic and robust during the CI/CD phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Rewrite my build script to include better error handling and logging, ensuring it fails gracefully if a dependency fails to install during deployment.

steps.title: Final Verification & Dry Run
steps.description: Simulate the deployment process locally to catch errors before pushing to the cloud.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dry-run test suite that mimics the production deployment environment and executes the build process to verify all configurations.

pro_tips: Always use a dedicated 'production-check' branch to isolate deployment experiments from your stable feature code.
Map your local Node/Python versions to the exact sub-version required by your cloud provider's runtime.
Use AI to write custom 'health check' endpoints that verify database connectivity immediately upon app start.
Treat your deployment configuration as code; version control your CI/CD yaml files just as strictly as your core logic.
When an error persists, ask the AI to explain the error in the context of your specific framework version, not just general programming.
content.next_steps: Once your deployment is successful, document the specific error and the fix in your portfolio. This demonstrates to employers that you possess real-world debugging and DevOps proficiency.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical leverage to overcome any deployment hurdle. Stop letting infrastructure errors stifle your creativity and start shipping code that users can actually interact with. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
